ontology.foodex2_description: Choux pastry / cream puff — baked filled pastry
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJM used as closest parent for choux-based filled pastry; no specific Hokkaido cream puff code exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue. This code represents the nearest defensible ancestor in the FoodEx2 hierarchy for baked filled choux pastry products.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept for cream puff or choux pastry identified in publicly available SNOMED CT browser.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a set of three crispy cream puffs (approximately 240 g total), comprising two Hokkaido milk cream puffs and one chocolate cream puff.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cream Puff, Homemade (SR Legacy ID 18964) and Eclair/Cream Puff with Custard Filling (Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact match for 'Crispy Hokkaido Cream Puff' in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database. Values adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry 18964 (cream puff, homemade, with custard filling) and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central eclair/cream puff entries. Input per_100g values (energy 312.5 kcal, protein 6.25 g, fat 20.83 g, carbohydrate 27.08 g) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy 18964 (energy ~335 kcal, protein 6.3 g, fat 20.8 g, carbohydrate 27.2 g per 100 g) and are retained with medium confidence. Saturated fat 12.5 g/100 g is plausible given heavy cream and butter content. Trans fat 0.63 g/100 g is plausible for butter-based pastry. Micronutrients (calcium 62.5 mg, iron 0.63 mg, cholesterol 104.17 mg) are consistent with USDA reference values for cream puff.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Éclair / choux pastry analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Hokkaido crispy cream puff exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 65 is estimated from the closest analogous food: choux pastry / éclair with cream filling, which appears in the International Tables of GI Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) with a GI of approximately 65 (medium). The high refined-flour content and cream filling of this product are compositionally similar. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (65 × 27.08 g/100g × 240 g serving / 100) = 65 × 65.0 g / 100 = 42.3.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (14.58 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (0.83 g/100 g) promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention; saturated fat (12.5 g/100 g) contributes to arterial stiffness.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to occasional small portions, as the high sugar and saturated fat content can exacerbate insulin resistance and vascular stiffness, indirectly worsening blood pressure control.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load (27.08 g/100 g total carbohydrate, 14.58 g/100 g sugar) with minimal dietary fibre (0.83 g/100 g) produces a rapid postprandial glucose spike; estimated GI of 65 and glycemic load of 42.3 per serving are clinically significant.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this food, as the high sugar and low fibre content will cause a rapid and sustained postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, restrict to a single small piece and pair with a high-fibre food to partially blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 12.5 g/100 g (from butter and heavy cream) substantially exceeds the 5 g/100 g cardiovascular threshold; trans fat at 0.63 g/100 g from butter further raises LDL cholesterol; cholesterol content is 104.17 mg/100 g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this food, as the high saturated fat and trans fat content from butter and cream will raise LDL cholesterol and promote atherogenesis; a 240 g serving delivers approximately 30 g saturated fat, which exceeds the entire recommended daily saturated fat limit for most adults.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat at 12.5 g/100 g from butter and cream exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold and promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity (TNF-alpha, IL-6); high sugar content further drives systemic inflammation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this food, as the high saturated fat from butter and cream is a known driver of inflammatory cytokine production, and the high sugar content further amplifies systemic inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Crispy Cream Puff Set (3 pieces, Assorted) is a Japanese-inspired dessert sold in Singapore, consisting of three choux pastry puffs with a characteristically crisp, crackled exterior shell and a rich, creamy filling. The set typically comprises two Hokkaido milk cream puffs — filled with lightly sweetened Hokkaido-origin dairy cream known for its exceptional richness and mild sweetness — and one chocolate cream puff filled with a cocoa-infused cream. The choux pastry shell is made from a cooked dough of flour, butter, eggs, and water, piped and baked until puffed and golden, then cooled and filled. The crispy exterior is achieved through a thin craquelin (cookie dough) topping baked onto the shell. Nutritionally, each 100 g delivers approximately 312.5 kcal, 6.25 g protein, 20.83 g fat (of which 12.5 g is saturated fat from butter and cream), and 27.08 g carbohydrate (14.58 g sugar). Dietary fibre is low at 0.83 g/100 g. The estimated glycemic index is 65 (medium), but the glycemic load per standard 3-puff serving (240 g) is approximately 42.3, which is clinically high. This food is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high saturated fat, high sugar, and low fibre profile. It is vegetarian but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs.
common_user_queries: What are the calories in a cream puff?
How much fat is in a Hokkaido cream puff?
What is the glycemic index of cream puff?
Is cream puff suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: No exact match for 'Crispy Hokkaido Cream Puff' found in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database. Nutrient values adapted from USDA SR Legacy cream puff entry (ID 18964) and are compositionally consistent with the described product. GI is an analogous estimate from choux pastry/éclair data in the International Tables of GI Values (Atkinson et al., 2008). Confidence is medium rather than low because the macronutrient profile is well-supported by the USDA reference and the product composition is well-described.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific cream puff product is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 18964 or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-17. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient data beyond calcium, iron, and cholesterol not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy reference for cream puff. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): cream puff contains no vitamin C-bearing ingredients; USDA SR Legacy entry 18964 reports 0 mg vitamin C. Input source listed 'https://foodo.sg/' as HPB Singapore source but the URL does not resolve to a specific nutrient entry for this product; nutrient values were therefore validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. The second portion entry in the input ('1 set of 6 cream puffs' at 6.0 g) appears to be a data entry error (6 g is implausibly small for 6 cream puffs); this portion has been excluded from the output. Only the primary portion (1 set of 3 cream puffs, 240 g) is retained. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI 65 × carbohydrate per serving 65.0 g) / 100 = 42.3. FoodEx2 match_type corrected from 'adapted' to 'closest_parent' in iteration 3 to conform to the allowed enumeration ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'].

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Identified that input source 'https://foodo.sg/' did not resolve to a specific HPB nutrient entry; replaced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 18964 as primary nutrient source with documented adaptation.
Iteration 1: Added Japanese local name in native script and romanized form to satisfy G6 multilingual requirement for Japanese cuisine.
Iteration 1: Added Chinese simplified and traditional names for Singapore market context.
Iteration 1: Constructed full GI entry using analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) for choux pastry/éclair.
Iteration 1: Corrected health_context to use 'type2_diabetes' machine key and 'Type 2 Diabetes' human_condition_name; added cardiovascular_disease condition.
Iteration 1: Documented all -1 fields and 0.0 vitamin_c in notes per g3 and g4 rules.
Iteration 1: Flagged and excluded erroneous second portion entry (6 g for 6 cream puffs) in notes.
Iteration 2: Added Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases to satisfy Singaporean dish multilingual requirements.
Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases array — all strings confirmed present verbatim.

Iteration 3: Corrected foodex2_match_type from 'adapted' to 'closest_parent' to conform to the allowed enumeration ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'], resolving the G5 ontology_honesty hard gate failure reported by the validator.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
